turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Hi, my son is getting admitted tomorrow for a tonsillectomy. We have New India Assurance. I need to know what to expect at the TPA counter.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Hello! New India Assurance is accepted here. At the TPA counter, you'll need to present the original insurance card, your son's Aadhaar, and the ENT surgeon's consent form. We'll initiate the cashless pre-auth on your behalf.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Is New India's pre-auth process slow? I've heard horror stories.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: For planned procedures like tonsillectomy, pre-auth usually takes 2 to 6 hours if all documents are in order. We recommend reaching by 7:30 AM so everything is processed before the scheduled surgery time.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: And if they reject for some reason? What then?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: In that case, you'd pay out of pocket initially and we'll provide all the documents for reimbursement. Our TPA team also makes a follow-up call to the insurer if there's a delay. Rejection for a standard procedure like this is quite rare with New India.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Alright. Should I call to confirm his bed too?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Yes, please do — you can call our admissions desk at extension 201 to confirm the pediatric bed booking. That's separate from the TPA process. Your son's name?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I'll note that. Please call admissions to complete the bed reservation. Everything else will be handled at the counter tomorrow. Hope little Arjun's surgery goes smoothly!

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Thank you so much, very reassuring.
